name: Agno
tagline: Python SDK for building, running, and managing AI agent platforms
seoTitle: Agno - Python Framework for AI Agent Platforms
metaDescription: Agno is an open-source Python SDK for building AI agents and running them as production services with storage, tools, scheduling, and RBAC.
overview: Agno (formerly Phidata) is an open-source Python SDK for building AI agents and running them in production. You define an agent with a model, a set of tools, and instructions in plain Python, then run it as a service with tracing, scheduling, and access control built in.

It is aimed at Python developers and teams who want to own their agent stack instead of relying on a hosted black box. You keep control of your data, context, tools, memory, and human-review loops, store sessions and traces in your own database, and deploy on any platform that runs containers.

As a general agent framework, Agno covers both the build step and the runtime. Beyond the core agent loop, it ships a production API, 100+ tool integrations, context providers for sources like Slack and Drive, human-approval gates, and OpenTelemetry-based observability.
features: Define agents in plain Python with a model, tools, instructions, and memory
Run agents as a production API with 50+ endpoints, SSE, and websockets
100+ pre-built tool integrations plus context providers (Slack, Drive, MCP, custom)
Human-approval gates to pause runs for confirmation and gate sensitive tools
Built-in observability via OpenTelemetry tracing, run history, and audit logs
Cron scheduling, background jobs, and container deploys to Docker, Railway, AWS, or GCP

gettingStarted.intro: Install Agno with a model provider, then define and run an agent in a short Python script.

steps.heading: Install Agno
steps.body: Install the agno package alongside a model provider client such as openai. The README's first-agent guide uses uv; plain pip also works.

steps.heading: Set your model provider key
steps.body: Export the API key for the provider you chose so the agent can call the model.

steps.heading: Build and run an agent
steps.body: Create an Agent with a model and instructions, then stream a response with print_response.
steps.code:
from agno.agent import Agent

agent = Agent(
    model="openai:gpt-5.5",
    instructions="You are a helpful assistant.",
    markdown=True,
)

agent.print_response("Say hello and tell me what you can do", stream=True)

useCases: Build a coding or data assistant that lives in Slack and answers from your own context sources
Stand up an internal agent platform with tracing, RBAC, and human approval that runs in your own cloud
Expose an agent to users over Slack, Telegram, WhatsApp, Discord, or A2A interfaces
Schedule recurring agent jobs (cron and background tasks) without extra infrastructure
